content:
In the world of software engineering, we often hear that “the code is the documentation.” But as projects scale, this becomes a dangerous myth. The reality is that documentation drift — where comments and code slowly stop matching — is a “silent killer” of developer velocity
Most teams postpone JSDoc updates until the end of a sprint, leading to stale doc blocks that are worse than missing ones because they actively mislead developers with “convincing lies”. To solve this, jsdoc-scribe was built as a purely deterministic, AST-driven utility to automate the generation, verification, and monitoring of documentation.
Here is a deep dive into the internal mechanics of how it works.

• The Engine: AST-Based Parsing (Not AI)
While modern tools rely on Large Language Models (LLMs) to “guess” what code does, jsdoc-scribe operates on a Zero AI Policy. AI often produces "hallucinated confidence," describing what a model thinks a function does rather than what is actually there.
Instead, jsdoc-scribe uses the TypeScript Compiler API to perform static analysis. It follows the standard compiler pipeline—specifically the Scanner and Parser stages—to convert source code into an Abstract Syntax Tree (AST).

• AST Truth: The tool extracts “facts” directly from the code structure: parameter names, type annotations, modifiers, and return statements.

• Syntactic Analysis: It proof-reads the code to ensure syntactical correctness without needing to execute or evaluate the logic.
2. Two Focused CLIs: A Documentation Pipeline
The package provides two primary command-line utilities that handle the lifecycle of documentation:

• gen-comments: This utility walks your source tree and inserts /** */ JSDoc blocks. It scaffolds tags like @param, @returns, and @async by reading the AST. It is designed to be non-destructive, meaning it leaves existing hand-written comments untouched unless forced.

• gen-docs: It takes the documented source and transforms it into a static, multi-page HTML documentation site. The output is lightweight — using only vanilla JS, CSS, and HTML — making it compatible with simple hosting like GitHub Pages without a build framework.
3. Verification: “Diff, Not a Guess”
The most critical feature for maintaining “honest” documentation is Drift Detection (--check-drift). The logic is surprisingly simple yet powerful:

• It extracts the AST Truth (what the code actually is).

• It extracts the Documentation Truth (what the existing JSDoc tags claim to be).

• It performs a set difference between the two.
If a developer renames a parameter in the code but forgets to update the comment, jsdoc-scribe flags the mismatch and returns an exit code 1, allowing it to serve as a Quality Gate in CI/CD pipelines.
4. Monitoring Code Health
With the release of version 2.0.1, the tool expanded its scope to include production-style monitoring for code quality. By integrating with code-multivitals (as an optional peer dependency), the documentation site transforms into a Code Health Dashboard.

• Metrics: It tracks Cyclomatic and Cognitive Complexity, Maintainability Index, and duplicate code detection.

• Holistic View: Just as teams monitor API latency or CPU usage, jsdoc-scribe treats code quality as a measurable asset that must be monitored continuously to prevent technical debt from becoming a bottleneck.
5. Performance and Scalability
Efficiency is a core technical constraint. Because it avoids network calls and AI model processing, the tool scales linearly with codebase size.

• Benchmarks: The tool can process approximately 233,000 lines of code in 4.17 seconds.

• Linear Scaling: Even on large enterprise projects with over 1,000 files, generation typically finishes in just over a second.
Final Thoughts
jsdoc-scribe is built on the belief that documentation shouldn't compete with development—it should be a deterministic byproduct of it. By using "AST truth" to keep docs synced, teams can bootstrap a documentation culture from the code they already have, turning an error-prone chore into a reliable engineering standard.
Try it today: npx jsdoc-scribe . --check-drift
